name: Breda 20/65 Mod. 35
summary: Italy's standard light anti-aircraft cannon. Used in ground-based and vehicle-mounted roles. Less sophisticated than the German Flak 38 but effective at low altitudes.

name: Motobomba FFF
summary: Air-dropped circling torpedo that spiralled in an expanding circle on the water surface until it struck a ship. Used by the Regia Aeronautica against Allied convoys in the Mediterranean.

name: Spoleto 45/5
summary: Italian recoilless anti-tank weapon developed late in the war. Saw limited operational use before Italy's armistice in 1943; subsequently some were used by RSI (Italian Social Republic) forces.
